what_it_does: Defines a static phone-screen HTML mockup for a single design piece, showing a preview artwork, take selector, adjustment dials, pinned notes, a call-to-action, and a bottom icon rail populated by runtime icons.
observations: Imports Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono from Google Fonts plus shared piece and phone stylesheets.
Inline CSS constructs the full visual treatment for the preview, numbered pins, take pills, and dial controls.
The preview area contains a titled composition with a hatched band, three color plates, a bottom hatch strip, and three numbered pin markers.
JavaScript injects three static pin rows into #pins and five runtime-generated icons into #rail via DNA.org.icon.
Several values, labels, positions, colors, and state indicators are hardcoded directly in the document.
standouts: Combines a miniature artwork preview with annotation pins and control dials in one mobile screen.
Uses material-like tokens such as gold, ochre, warm, cool, nested, page, line, and charcoal to frame the piece as a design substrate.
Represents review state through takes, pins, keeper/cutter labels, and adjustable qualities rather than through form-heavy controls.
conventions: Uses shared CSS variables for spacing, radius, type scale, color, and borders.
Keeps the page as a single-file prototype with inline component-specific styling and inline data.
Follows a phone-shell convention with status bar, back control, section header, rows, action button, and bottom rail.
Uses short class names for local styling and relies on shared classes such as screen, sbar, p-back, sec, p-rows, p-act, and p-rail.
concerns: Static HTML and inline JavaScript make the screen easy to view but tightly couple content, styling, and behavior.
Pin row HTML is assembled with template strings without escaping, which is acceptable for the current static literals but fragile if data becomes external.
The preview pins and dial values are visually positioned with hardcoded inline styles, so content changes may require manual layout adjustment.
External font loading and /surface runtime/style dependencies are required for the intended rendering.
notes: Brief this as a mobile piece-focus prototype that frames a design artifact through preview, takes, dials, and pinned review notes.
questions: Are the take labels intended to be interactive or only visual state?
Do keeper and cutter represent fixed review categories or placeholder labels?
Should dial values eventually map to real piece parameters?
Is the bottom rail meant to navigate between app sections or only establish visual context?
purpose_vs_actual: The title states a screen with one piece in focus, and the file implements a focused mobile screen for one piece with preview, annotations, take state, and adjustment controls.
intention_signals: It seems to be trying to show how a single design piece can be inspected, annotated, compared across takes, and adjusted through named qualitative controls.
novelty: A compact design-review screen that treats an artwork-like substrate as something with pins, takes, keeper/cutter judgments, and dialed qualities.
contribution: Contributes a file-local model for a piece-detail surface: visual artifact preview, annotation pins, qualitative adjustment dials, take selection, pinned-note rows, and a navigation rail within a phone frame.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML mobile prototype for a single design piece, combining preview artwork, numbered pins, take pills, adjustment dials, pin rows, action button, and icon rail.
intention_for_embedding: Designed to express a focused piece-review workflow where a visual substrate can be annotated, tuned, and situated among app navigation controls.
